arguments.task: Open and read official Vonage first-party pages that support: product description, authentication methods, pricing or access tier, API reference/surface type, rate limits, and whether an MCP server exists. Prefer canonical developer.vonage.com or developer.nexmo.com pages and any official GitHub pages if linked by Vonage. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field. Do not rely on search snippets. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for content to load and capture the visible text.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official Vonage sources.

raw_result.description: Communications platform offering APIs for messaging, voice, video, verification, and related customer communications use cases.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official authentication docs describe two methods across Vonage APIs: API Key and Secret for some APIs, and JWT for others. Evidence gathered did not verify OAuth2, Basic, or other first-party auth schemes for the general API platform in this pass.
raw_result.gating_reason: Vonage publishes public developer documentation and public communications API pricing pages, indicating self-serve developer access rather than partner/contact-sales-only gating.

api_surface.breadth: Public docs show a broad HTTP API surface across messaging, voice, video, verification, and other communications products. This pass did not verify GraphQL evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server documentation or official MCP offering was verified in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The browser task navigated toward an official rate-limits page, but no supporting page text was captured in the gathered evidence, so specific rate-limit details are unverified in this pass.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer docs clearly organize product areas and authentication concepts, and public pricing is available for communications APIs.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured field uncertainty remains for mcp_exists and parts of auth/access verification because the gathered evidence did not include a read official MCP page or a captured pricing/auth page excerpt sufficient to fully exclude other documented auth methods or confirm all self-serve signup conditions.
